How's it going everyone? In this video we're going to cover at least the basics of getting your front account and connecting your Twilio number. Now one thing that's very important with front is that if you don't want to pay full price you need to use the buttons below the video to sign up for your front account. The reason for that is we have a partnership with front to offer all of our partners a 20% off discount and that's for life. So this 54-40 per month you see is actually 20% off the normal price. Now with the monthly plan you're paying month to month, there are no contracts or commitments with it. If you go with the yearly it is a bit more upfront but you're actually getting the same amount as two months free. So if you're going to keep front for at least a little while yearly is almost always a better deal. So use one of the links below this video either the monthly or the yearly whichever one you prefer and then I'll take you to one of these pages, go into your name, your company, your admin's email if you want to use a different email and then the company name. So I'm going to go and enter in some information here. Once you hit pay now it's going to take you right to this page. It's going to set up the payment directly so you'll pay your information right away. Now I've already set up a trial account, that way I don't have to use my credit card information to sign up for this. Once you pay it's going to take you to this onboarding process. It's going to ask for a little bit of information and once you're first and last name, if it may skip this it just kind of depends. It'll have you choose a username and that's going to be kind of like your internal name so you want to use something that people will be able to recognize as you. That's very important if you're going to have other people use your front account as well, that way they're able to see it and they're able to go with it and find you and assign messages and same thing. Everything looks good there, you'll hit continue. It should ask you what industry you're in, what your role is, and you want to choose the right one here so health and fitness and CEO owner or whichever role fits the best for you. Hit continue. It's going to ask you to go and get your team messages in front. So you want to go and name this inbox whichever one you prefer. I'm going to call it the 123 Fitness Twilio and that's because I'm going to hook up our Twilio phone number with this. So once I put in that name there, I'm going to hit connect accounts and it's going to ask you to connect an account to your team inbox either email, Facebook or Twilio. This is one of the great parts about front is you can actually have multiple inboxes inside of there, one for each one. That way you have one place to log in and see all of your communication, whether it's through that phone number, that Twilio number, whether it's through Facebook or through email, you can use front for all of it. So I'm going to go and choose Twilio SMS. It's going to ask for the phone number. Now you'll need to go ahead and open that up. I'm going to go and open a new tab. I'm going to go back to Twilio. That's twilio.com. Now if you still have your Twilio window open, you're good to go. You don't have to go through it. Once you get logged into Twilio, it has this account SID and this auth token. If you notice to the right of it, it has this copy button. It says these two little pages. It says copy when you move your mouse over it. I'm going to go ahead and hit copy for the account SID. I'm going to click the tab to go back to front. I'm going to click where it asks for the account SID and I'm going to do control V to paste it. I'm going to head back to Twilio. I'm going to click this copy again right next to auth token this time. Once you copy it, I'm going to head back to the front page. Click where it asks for OAuth token. I'm going to paste it. I'm also going to get my phone number. I'm heading back to Twilio. I'm going to select my phone number and copy it. I'm back to front and paste it. You should have all three pieces of information in here. All three are available from the main dashboard in Twilio. As soon as you log in, it should take you right here. You get your phone number, your account SID, and your auth token all from the same places. Again, to copy your account SID or auth token, you just click that little button to the right of it, to copy it, and then paste it. If that looks great, go ahead and hit this connect Twilio button right there. It says this phone number has a webhook URL associated with it and the account setting will be overwritten. Do you wish to continue? This is the right phone number and this is the first time you're connecting it with Twilio in front. That's perfectly fine to get this message. Go ahead and hit yes on it. Now that we've gotten that connected, go ahead and hit this blue continue button. It's going to have you set up your team. Now if you want other people to be able to log into front and manage this account with you, this is where you're going to put in their email addresses. If you don't have anybody else you want to be able to access at this time, go ahead and hit the skip for now. This tour video is a great place to go and start. It's only about two minutes and 45 seconds. I'm going to give you a brief rundown of where everything is in front. I'm going to skip watching it and hit this take me to front button right there. I do recommend you watch that video. If you notice, it's already started pulling in my email addresses. Over here we have this 123 fitness and team inbox. This is where my phone number is. If I go to inbox one, this is actually the phone number. If somebody were to text my new Twilio number, then I would actually see it right here right away. I would be able to respond and front would send that message to Twilio and out to them. Front's a great place to consolidate all of your different communication channels into one place. You can invite others so they're able to help manage it with you. It's a really great tool to use if you want. One thing that a lot of people ask about front is importing contacts to it. Front's actually taken our feedback and made that process really easy. Say you went to your CRM and you want to bring in a whole bunch of contacts in the front, so that way if they message your number, you see their name and information right away. At the bottom left of front, we have this contacts button right there. So when you click on contacts, it'll take you to this page. And at the top right, we have this blue little person with a plus sign on it. Click the arrow next to that and click import to my contacts. It's going to take you to this import contacts screen. It tells you what the headings need to be. So when you export it, it should be fine. It's probably in those settings. Otherwise you may need to make a couple changes to that file that you export from your CRM with all of your contacts. Otherwise, go ahead and hit choose file and you're able to choose that CSV file and hit import CSV. And it'll bring all of those contacts in there. So just a quick review. We walked you through using one of the referral links for front, so that way you get 20% off either the monthly or the yearly. Early is actually going to be the same as getting two months for free. So I definitely recommend that if you're looking to save some money in the long run. We went ahead and enter in the basic company information. And then once it got to the team inbox section, we went ahead and told it to connect our Twilio account with a phone number. We went to Twilio and we got our phone number, our account SID and our auth token. We copied them there and put them where it said to in the front set up. Once that was all done, we were able to get to front and we had it set up. And then we also covered how to import contacts into front. So that way, again, the phone numbers that you get messages from can be associated with the people that you're messaging. And so you can do up to 3000 people at one time. If you have any questions, always let us know. I hope you enjoyed this video and hope you have a great rest of your day.
